seoTitle: Shelburne vs Shell Pocket — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Shelburne and Shell Pocket. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Shelburne vs Shell Pocket: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shelburne and Shell Pocket.
heading: Shelburne vs Shell Pocket — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shelburne, Queensland and Shell Pocket, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shell Pocket has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Shelburne (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shelburne or Shell Pocket safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shelburne has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Shell Pocket scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shelburne scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Shell Pocket scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shelburne or Shell Pocket?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shell Pocket has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
